message:
feat(manifest,readiness,mcp): add `z:project:*` wildcard policy selector for zone allowlists

Manifests need a way to grant a connector access to *all* concrete
project zones (`z:project:<id>`) without enumerating each one and
without weakening the `ZoneId` grammar (which deliberately rejects `*`
so concrete zone IDs stay strict). Introduce a new `ZonePattern` type
restricted to two shapes — concrete `ZoneId`s or the documented
`z:project:*` selector — and thread it through both manifest parsing
and the consumers that match incoming zones against allowlists.

`fcp-manifest` (`crates/fcp-manifest/src/lib.rs`)

* New public type `ZonePattern(Arc<str>)` representing a validated zone
  policy selector. Implements `TryFrom<String>`, `From<ZoneId>`,
  `FromStr`, `Display`, `AsRef<str>`, plus cross-equality with
  `ZoneId`. Serialization uses `try_from = "String"` / `into = "String"`
  so manifests round-trip the existing TOML/JSON form.
* `ZonePattern::PROJECT_WILDCARD = "z:project:*"`, `is_project_wildcard`,
  and `matches(&ZoneId)` give callers a single canonical place to ask
  "does this selector accept this zone?".
* New `ZonePatternError` variant tree: `UnsupportedWildcard` for any
  `*`-bearing string that isn't the documented selector,
  `ZoneId(#[from] ZoneIdError)` for everything else, so we don't paper
  over real `ZoneId` validation failures.
* `ZonesSection::allowed_sources` and `ZonesSection::allowed_targets`
  switch from `Vec<ZoneId>` to `Vec<ZonePattern>`. `home` and
  `forbidden` stay as concrete `ZoneId`s — wildcards in those fields
  would be either meaningless (home) or operationally dangerous
  (forbidden).
* Doc-comment fix on `PolicySection::require_attestation_expiry`:
  backtick `expires_at` so it renders as code.

`fcp-manifest` tests (`tests/no_mock_integration.rs`,
`tests/manifest_golden.rs`)

* `zones_allow_project_wildcard_policy_selector` parses a manifest with
  `allowed_sources = ["z:work", "z:project:*"]` and
  `allowed_targets = ["z:project:*"]` and asserts the wildcard accepts
  `z:project:alpha` and *rejects* the home zone (`z:work`) — the
  wildcard is project-scoped, not universal.
* `zones_reject_unsupported_wildcard_policy_selector` confirms
  `allowed_sources = ["z:*"]` produces an "unsupported zone wildcard"
  error.
* Backtick fix in `manifest_golden.rs` doc comment to match other
  identifier callouts.

`fwc` readiness (`crates/fwc/src/readiness.rs`)

* `DiscoveredConnector::matches_zone` now delegates to a new
  `zone_selector_matches` helper that accepts either an exact match or
  a `ZonePattern::PROJECT_WILDCARD` selector against any
  `z:project:*` concrete zone.
* `supported_zones` collection rewritten to insert `home` into a
  `BTreeSet<String>` rather than chain it into the iterator — clearer
  intent and avoids constructing the home `ZoneId` twice through the
  iterator chain.
* `zone_selector_matches` validates wildcard candidates by parsing the
  incoming zone string through `fcp_core::ZoneId::try_from` first, so a
  malformed `z:project:` prefix can't sneak through the wildcard branch.

`fwc` MCP server (`crates/fwc/src/serve_mcp.rs`)

* Same `zone_selector_matches` helper, ported into the MCP tool path so
  `McpToolDefinition::matches_zone` and `zone_registry_for_tools`
  honor `z:project:*` wildcards. Tools advertised against the wildcard
  are now registered against the *concrete* `default_zone` of the
  scoped registry, which is what zone-filtered `tools/list` calls
  expect.
* New `sample_project_tool` fixture and
  `handle_tools_list_respects_project_wildcard_zone_filter` test
  covering the end-to-end path: a tool advertising `z:project:*` is
  surfaced when the server is filtered to `z:project:alpha`, and a
  `z:private` tool is correctly excluded.
